channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Madhya Pradesh HC permits adult woman to live independently with partner of choice
item.description: In a significant judgment, the Madhya Pradesh High Court's Gwalior bench allowed a 19-year-old married woman to live independently with a man of her choosing. The court acted on a habeas corpus petiti...

item.title: Artemis 2 crew captures first human views of Moon’s Orientale Basin during lunar mission
item.description: NASA’s Artemis 2 astronauts have taken humanity’s first close look at the Orientale Basin on the Moon — a striking crater often referred to as the lunar ‘Grand Canyon’. The crew, consisting of America...

item.title: Government halts second fuel shipment amid import and licensing standoff
item.description: In the continuing fuel saga, Kenya’s Energy Cabinet Secretary Opiyo Wandayi has confirmed that another shipment of petroleum has been stopped from docking at the Port of Mombasa. This comes barely wee...

item.title: Canal+ outlines MultiChoice turnaround with field expansion and Showmax closure
item.description: Canal+ is revamping MultiChoice through a back-to-basics strategy aimed at boosting sales growth, safeguarding local content, restructuring the workforce, and handling regulatory oversight. Central to...

item.title: Taiwan’s KMT leader Cheng Li-wun plans rare China visit seeking cross-strait stability
item.description: Taiwan’s main opposition leader, Cheng Li-wun, head of the Kuomintang (KMT), is set to visit China for a rare trip aimed at promoting cross-strait peace. This will mark the first such visit by a sitti...

item.title: Apple Extends Device-Based Age Checks to Singapore and South Korea with Additional Verification Options
item.description: Apple has extended its device-level age verification feature—first introduced in the United Kingdom—to two new markets: Singapore and South Korea. According to the report by 9to5Mac, this system requi...

item.title: Tanzania Advocates for Stronger African Economic Self-Reliance
item.description: During the 58th Conference of African Ministers for Finance, Planning and Economic Development and Central Bank Governors in Tangier, Morocco, Tanzania's Finance Minister, Ambassador Khamis Mussa Omar...

item.title: New York Islanders replace Patrick Roy with Peter DeBoer amid late-season slump
item.description: The New York Islanders have fired head coach Patrick Roy after the team’s recent losing streak put their playoff hopes in jeopardy. First-year general manager Mathieu Darche made the decision with onl...

item.title: DOTr enforces stricter measures after airport theft incident involving OTS staff
item.description: The Department of Transportation (DOTr) has announced stricter oversight of the Office for Transportation Security (OTS) following the dismissal of three personnel accused of stealing money from an Au...

item.title: CJI Constitutes Nine-Judge Bench to Address Women's Rights Across Religions
item.description: In a landmark move reflecting the principle that 'justice must not only be done, but must also be seen to be done,' Chief Justice of India Surya Kant has set up a nine-judge bench to examine contentio...

item.title: Ugandan Law Student Injured While Portraying Jesus in Good Friday Reenactment
item.description: A Good Friday religious procession in Uganda took an unfortunate turn when Dennis Zziwa, a second-year law student at Kampala International University, was injured while portraying Jesus Christ during...

item.title: Iran Grants Safe Passage to Philippine-Flagged Ships Amid Global Oil Crisis
item.description: Following Iran’s assurance of 'safe passage' for Philippine-flagged ships through the Strait of Hormuz, the Philippines has seen reduced risks to its fuel supply but not an immediate improvement in fu...

item.title: Confusion over Pera enforcement of 10pm curbs on exempt businesses and sports in Lahore
item.description: In Lahore, the Punjab Enforcement Regulatory Authority (Pera) has been reportedly overstepping its mandate by enforcing 10pm closure timings on sectors that are actually exempt, including sports facil...

item.title: Four Palestinians killed in northern Gaza amid ceasefire tensions
item.description: On Sunday, an Israeli airstrike struck a group of Palestinians in the northern Gaza Strip, near Jaffa Street and the Darraj neighbourhood in Gaza City, killing four people and injuring others, accordi...

item.title: Overview of Australia's weather for Monday, April 6
item.description: The national weather forecast for Monday, April 6, 2026, provides Australians with an update on expected conditions across the country. Residents should anticipate variable weather patterns, with some...

item.title: Gulf Conflict Triggers Massive Withdrawal from Pakistan T-Bills
item.description: The ongoing Gulf War is causing significant economic strain for Pakistan, as foreign investor confidence in domestic treasury bills (T-bills) drops sharply. According to State Bank of Pakistan (SBP) d...

item.title: Steve Backshall observes two of the UK's last surviving orcas off Cornwall
item.description: Wildlife explorer Steve Backshall expressed immense joy after spotting two of the last surviving orcas in the UK, named John Coe and Aquarius. These male killer whales belong to the West Coast Communi...

item.title: Teen dies in M25 crash while driving father under influence near KwaMashu
item.description: A heartbreaking accident occurred on the M25 near KwaMashu in KwaZulu-Natal, where a 14-year-old boy lost his life after the vehicle he was driving overturned several times. According to Reaction Unit...

item.title: Germany introduces new travel rules for men aged 17–45 as part of military reform
item.description: Germany don bring new travel law wey go affect men wey dey between 17 and 45 years old. From January 1, 2026, any man wey wan travel outside Germany for more than three months must apply for permissio...

item.title: Majembe Earns Big Rewards After Winning Vurugu Boxing Match
item.description: Kenyan boxer Potifus Odipo, better known by his ring name 'Majembe,' enjoyed a major payday after defeating Ferdinand Omondi, also known as 'Mbavu Destroyer,' during the Vurugu boxing match held on Ap...
